steps.title: Compare both sides
steps.content: The equation becomes $60x - 90 = 60x - 90$. Both sides are identical for every value of $x$. This means the equation is always true — it has infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Exactly one: The variable cancels completely, so there is no unique solution. ✗
  • Choice B — Exactly two: A linear equation cannot have exactly two solutions. ✗
  • Choice C — Infinitely many: The equation is an identity; every real number satisfies it. ✓
  • Choice D — Zero: Zero solutions would require a contradiction like $0 = 1$; instead we get $0 = 0$. ✗
C is correct.
